title: Accreditation of Foreign Principals/ Employers and Projects
content: The 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs provide guidelines for the accreditation of foreign principals, employers, and projects. Accreditation is a mandatory process to ensure that foreign employers and their projects meet the standards and requirements set by the Philippine Overseas Employment Administration (POEA) before they can hire Filipino workers. The process involves submission of necessary documents, evaluation of the employer's legitimacy, and approval by the POEA. Accredited foreign principals and projects are listed in the POEA registry, which serves as a reference for recruitment agencies and OFWs.
